lib\\legal-content.ts.head:
export const PRIVACY_POLICY_TEXT = `**Privacy Policy**

**Effective Date:** March 22, 2026
**Last Updated:** March 22, 2026

PrepVista (â€œPrepVista,â€ â€œwe,â€ â€œus,â€ or â€œourâ€) is committed to protecting your privacy and handling personal information in a responsible, transparent, and secure manner. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, receive, use, process, store, disclose, transfer, retain, and otherwise handle information when you access, visit, register for, interact with, or use our website, platform, applications, products, features, content, communications, and related services, including interview practice, personalized question generation, coaching, performance reporting, subscriptions, account management, customer support, and any related functionality we make available from time to time (collectively, the â€œServicesâ€). This Privacy Policy is intended to help you understand what information we collect, why we collect it, how we use it, when it may be shared, how long it may be retained, what rights and choices you may have, and how we seek to safeguard it. By accessing or using the Services, you acknowledge that you have read and understood this Privacy Policy and agree to the practices described in it, subject to applicable law.

We may collect information directly from you, automatically through your use of the Services, and in certain cases from authorized third parties or service providers. The information we collect depends on how you use PrepVista, the features you access, the information you choose to provide, the permissions you grant, and the transactions you complete. Information you provide directly may include your name, email address, account credentials, profile information, communication preferences, subscription choices, support messages, uploaded documents, feedback, form responses, and any other information or content you voluntarily submit through the Services. If you create an account, we may collect information necessary to establish, authenticate, maintain, and secure that account. If you choose to sign in through a third-party authentication provider, we may receive limited profile or account information associated with that provider, subject to the permissions you authorize and the settings of that provider.

As part of the Services, you may upload resumes, CVs, career documents, or similar materials for personalization and interview preparation purposes. When you submit such materials, we may collect, process, analyze, and store the content contained in those materials in order to provide and improve the Services. This may include information such as education history, professional experience, work samples, projects, skills, certifications, achievements, and other career-related details that you choose to include. We may use this information to tailor interview questions, personalize practice sessions, improve the relevance of feedback, adapt question difficulty or subject matter to your background, and provide outputs that are more closely aligned with your profile, goals, and experience. The quality and relevance of some features may depend in part on the accuracy, completeness, and appropriateness of the information you choose to provide.

When you participate in interview sessions, voice-based interactions, coaching flows, evaluation features, feedback modules, or related user experiences, we may collect and process information associated with those interactions. This may include prompts shown to you, answers you provide, transcripts derived from your responses, interview mode selections, timing information, categories of questions, evaluation results, scores, comparative performance insights, progress history, strengths and weaknesses identified by the system, recommendations, coaching feedback, and other session-related information. If a feature involves spoken responses, your spoken input may be converted into text and processed as part of the Services. If a feature requests access to your microphone or camera, such access is requested solely to provide that particular feature or user-selected functionality. Depending on the design of the specific feature, some information may be processed temporarily, locally, or in connection with the relevant session experience in order to support voice interaction, focus monitoring, realistic simulation, personalization, or related service functionality. We do not use camera or microphone access for purposes unrelated to delivering the feature you have chosen to use.

We may also collect technical, device, and usage information automatically when you use the Services. This may include browser type, device type, operating system, language settings, IP address, approximate location information inferred from technical data, timestamps, pages or screens viewed, features accessed, buttons clicked, referral information, session duration, authentication events, account activity, reports viewed or downloaded, interviews started or completed, plan usage, quota consumption, cookies, local storage data, session identifiers, logs, crash information, diagnostic data, and similar information. We collect and use this information to operate the Services, maintain security, authenticate users, preserve session integrity, detect suspicious activity, enforce subscription limits, monitor performance, troubleshoot problems, improve reliability, analyze usage trends, understand how features are used, refine product design, and support overall business operations. We may also generate aggregated, statistical, anonymized, or de-identified information derived from this data for analytics, reporting, research, benchmarking, testing, service optimization, and lawful business improvement purposes.

test_50_questions_validation.py.head:
"""
COMPREHENSIVE 50-QUESTION VALIDATION
=====================================
Generates 50 deterministic follow-up questions across ALL plans (Free, Pro, Career),
ALL question families, MULTIPLE resume types, and varied answer signals.

Each question is validated for:
  1. NOT empty — the function must return a question
  2. NO vague phrases — "that work", "that project", "there", etc. are banned
     unless the specific subject is already named in the same sentence
  3. GROUNDED — references a specific project, skill, role, or education detail
  4. USER CLARITY — question is self-contained (user knows exactly what it refers to)
  5. STRUCTURE INTACT — original return types and call signatures preserved

This test ensures 100% accuracy and deployment readiness.
signatures: def test_question_is_grounded(plan, builder_key, previous_q, user_answer, resume_fn, label):

"""Standalone verification script for interviewer upgrades.
Tests the pure functions without requiring asyncpg or other database dependencies.
Run with: python tests/verify_upgrades.py
"""
import sys
import os
sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8')

# Ensure project root is in sys.path
